name: Canadian Space Agency (CSA)

overview.value: The Canadian Space Agency (CSA) was established in March 1989 as the federal agency responsible for managing all of Canada's civil space-related activities. Its mission is advancing the knowledge of space through science and using its discoveries for the good of Canadians and all of humanity.

focus.value: Federal agency responsible for managing all of Canada's civil space-related activities

notes: Added 2026-07-08 per Florian (anti-spoof option B: registry-recorded website gives CSA pages an official_record path). Headquarters Longueuil, Quebec per asc-csa.gc.ca/eng/about/; no headquarters field on OrgProfile. The Canadian Space Agency Act (S.C. 1990, c. 13) formalized in 1990 an agency the CSA's own page states was established in March 1989; the agency's own stated year is used.
